articles.title: Women perceive AI as riskier than men do, study finds
articles.titleKo: 여성이 남성보다 AI를 더 위험하다고 인식한다는 연구 결과
articles.summary: A new study finds that women are more skeptical of artificial intelligence than men. Driven by higher risk aversion and greater exposure to job displacement, this cautious approach could inadvertently widen gender inequalities in the workplace.
articles.aiSummary:
A new study reveals that women perceive artificial intelligence as riskier compared to men, driven primarily by higher risk aversion and greater concerns about job displacement. Although specific numerical data and sample sizes were not detailed in the report, the research demonstrates a significant gender gap in AI perception that could have substantial workplace implications. If women remain cautious or reluctant to adopt AI technologies, this cautious approach may inadvertently widen existing gender inequalities in the workplace and limit their opportunities in tech-related fields. The study highlights the importance of understanding and addressing women's concerns about AI technology to promote gender equality in the digital age. Policymakers and educators should consider developing targeted interventions to increase women's confidence and engagement with AI. However, the research may have limitations in analyzing specific risk factors and controlling for various demographic variables that could influence perceptions.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
최근 연구에 따르면 여성들이 남성보다 인공지능(AI)을 더 위험하다고 인식하는 것으로 나타났습니다. 이 연구는 여성의 높은 위험회피 성향과 직업 대체에 대한 더 큰 우려가 AI에 대한 회의적 태도를 유발한다는 것을 보여줍니다. 구체적인 수치와 표본 규모는 제시되지 않았으나, 이러한 성별 인식 차이가 향후 AI 관련 교육 및 취업 기회 접근에서 여성의 불리함으로 이어질 수 있다는 점이 핵심입니다. 여성이 AI 기술 채택을 꺼린다면 직업 시장에서의 성평등이 더욱 악화될 우려가 있습니다. 이 연구는 기술 분야의 성별 격차를 해소하는 데 있어 여성의 AI에 대한 우려를 적극적으로 이해하고 해결해야 함을 시사합니다. 다만, 연구에서 위험 인식의 구체적인 원인 분석과 다양한 인구통계학적 변수가 충분히 다루어지지 않았을 가능성이 있습니다.

articles.title: Do we drink because we feel down, or feel down because we drink? A new study has the answer
articles.titleKo: 우리가 기분이 안 좋아서 술을 마시는 걸까, 아니면 술을 마셔서 기분이 안 좋아지는 걸까? 새로운 연구가 그 답을 제시하다
articles.summary: A yearlong observational study reveals that preserving emotional well-being might be the secret to naturally curbing alcohol consumption over time. The findings show that mental health heavily influences drinking habits, rather than the other way around.
articles.aiSummary:
This research aimed to clarify the causal relationship between alcohol consumption and mental health. Researchers conducted a one-year longitudinal observational study, collecting data through continuous monitoring of participants. The findings revealed that deteriorating mental health leads to increased drinking, rather than alcohol consumption worsening mental health. In other words, people tend to drink more when experiencing negative emotions. This discovery suggests that alcohol addiction treatment and prevention programs should focus more on mental health management. However, the study relied on self-reported data collection methods, and participant characteristics may have been limited in scope.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 음주와 정신건강 사이의 인과관계를 밝히기 위해 진행되었습니다. 연구진은 1년에 걸쳐 참가자들을 지속적으로 관찰하는 종단 연구 설계를 통해 데이터를 수집했습니다. 연구 결과는 음주량이 정신건강을 악화시키는 것이 아니라, 오히려 정신건강 악화가 음주량 증가로 이어진다는 것을 보여주었습니다. 즉, 감정 상태가 좋지 않을 때 사람들이 더 많이 마시는 경향이 있다는 의미입니다. 이 발견은 음주 중독 치료 및 예방 프로그램이 정신건강 관리에 더욱 집중해야 한다는 점을 시사합니다. 다만 이 연구는 자가보고 방식의 데이터 수집에 의존했으며, 참가자 특성이 제한적일 수 있다는 한계가 있습니다.

##

articles.title: Compile to Compress: Boosting Formal Theorem Provers by Compiler Outputs
articles.titleKo: 컴파일에서 압축으로: 컴파일러 출력을 활용한 형식 정리 증명기 성능 향상
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.18587v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated significant potential in formal theorem proving, yet state-of-the-art performance often necessitates prohibitive test-time compute via massive roll-outs or extended context windows. In this work, we address this scalability bottleneck by exploiting an informative structure in formal verification: the observation that compilers map a vast space of diverse proof attempts to a compact set of structured failure modes. We introduce a learning-to-refine framework that leverages this compression to perform
articles.aiSummary: This research addresses the scalability bottleneck of Large Language Models (LLMs) in formal theorem proving, which requires prohibitive test-time computation through massive roll-outs or extended context windows. The research team proposes a "learning-to-refine framework" based on the key observation that compilers compress diverse proof attempts into a compact set of structured failure modes. By leveraging compiler outputs as informative feedback, this approach enables improved theorem proving performance without requiring extensive computational resources such as multiple sampling attempts or long context windows. The significance of this work lies in exploiting the structural properties of formal verification to enhance AI learning efficiency substantially. This methodology promises to significantly improve the practicality of automated theorem proving, with important implications for mathematics and software verification. However, the study may be limited to specific compiler environments, and further validation is needed regarding generalization to diverse theorem-proving problems.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 본 연구는 대규모 언어모델(LLM)이 형식 정리 증명(formal theorem proving)에서 뛰어난 성능을 보이지만 막대한 계산 비용이 필요한 문제를 해결하기 위해 수행되었습니다. 연구팀은 컴파일러가 다양한 증명 시도들을 구조화된 실패 패턴으로 압축한다는 원리에 착안하여, "학습-개선 프레임워크(learning-to-refine framework)"를 제안했습니다. 이 방식은 컴파일러 출력(compiler outputs)의 정보를 활용함으로써 대량의 시도(roll-outs)나 긴 맥락 창(context window) 없이도 증명 성능을 개선할 수 있습니다. 연구의 핵심은 형식 검증의 구조적 특성을 AI 학습에 활용하여 계산 효율성을 획기적으로 높인 점입니다. 이 접근법은 자동화된 정리 증명의 실용성을 크게 향상시켜 수학 및 소프트웨어 검증 분야에서 중요한 의미를 갖습니다. 다만, 연구는 특정 컴파일러 환경에서의 테스트에 제한될 수 있으며, 다양한 증명 문제로의 일반화 가능성에 대한 추가 검증이 필요합니다.

keyPoints: **계산 효율성 개선** / **Improved Computational Efficiency**: 컴파일러 출력을 활용하여 대량의 시도나 긴 맥락 창 없이 정리 증명 성능 향상 - Leverages compiler outputs to enhance theorem proving performance without massive roll-outs or extended context windows

**구조적 정보 활용** / **Exploitation of Structural Information**: 컴파일러가 다양한 증명 시도를 실패 패턴으로 압축하는 특성을 AI 학습에 적용 - Applies the principle that compilers compress diverse proof attempts into structured failure modes to AI learning

**실무 적용 가능성** / **Practical Applicability**: 자동화된 정리 증명의 실용화를 가능하게 하여 수학, 소프트웨어 검증 등 다양한 분야에서 활용 가능 - Enables practical application of automated theorem proving across mathematics, software verification, and related fields

articles.title: Easy Samples Are All You Need: Self-Evolving LLMs via Data-Efficient Reinforcement Learning
articles.titleKo: 쉬운 샘플만으로 충분하다: 데이터 효율적 강화학습을 통한 자기진화 대규모언어모델

articles.aiSummary:
This study addresses the challenge of enabling large language models (LLMs) to learn efficiently through self-improvement mechanisms. Traditional approaches either require high annotation costs or suffer from model collapse and reward hacking issues. The research team, inspired by cognitive learning theory, proposes "EasyRL," a novel method that simulates human cognitive acquisition curves by progressively learning from easy to difficult samples. The core advantage of EasyRL is improving data efficiency while significantly reducing annotation costs compared to existing supervised approaches. Unlike voting-based or entropy-based reward systems, EasyRL mitigates reward hacking problems that plague current unsupervised paradigms. This approach offers a practical solution to enhance LLM self-improvement efficiency with potential widespread applications. However, the study would benefit from additional empirical validation across diverse tasks and domains.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 대규모언어모델(LLM)이 효율적으로 스스로 학습할 수 있는 방법을 개발하는 것을 목표로 합니다. 기존 방식들은 높은 annotation 비용이 필요하거나 모델 붕괴 문제가 있었습니다. 연구팀은 인지학습이론에서 영감을 받아 'EasyRL'이라는 새로운 방법을 제안했으며, 이는 인간의 학습곡선을 모방하여 신뢰할 수 있는 쉬운 샘플부터 점진적으로 어려운 샘플로 학습을 진행합니다. 이 접근법의 핵심은 데이터 효율성을 높이면서도 annotation 비용을 크게 줄일 수 있다는 것입니다. EasyRL은 기존의 투표 기반 또는 엔트로피 기반 보상 방식의 문제점인 보상 해킹(reward hacking)을 해결합니다. 본 연구는 LLM의 자율 학습 효율성을 획기적으로 개선할 수 있는 실용적 방법을 제시하며, 앞으로 더 다양한 실험을 통한 검증이 필요합니다.

##

Traditional approaches either require high annotation costs or suffer from model collapse and reward hacking issues. The research team, inspired by cognitive learning theory, proposes "EasyRL," a novel method that simulates human cognitive acquisition curves by progressively learning from easy to difficult samples. The core advantage of EasyRL is improving data efficiency while significantly reducing annotation costs compared to existing supervised approaches. Unlike voting-based or entropy-based reward systems, EasyRL mitigates reward hacking problems that plague current unsupervised paradigms. This approach offers a practical solution to enhance LLM self-improvement efficiency with potential widespread applications. However, the study would benefit from additional empirical validation across diverse tasks and domains.

##
keyPoints: **인지학습 기반 접근**: 인간의 학습과정을 모방하여 쉬운 샘플부터 어려운 샘플로 점진적으로 학습 진행 / Cognitive learning theory-inspired approach that mimics human learning progression from easy to difficult samples
**비용 효율성**: Annotation 비용을 크게 감소시키면서도 데이터 효율성을 극대화 / Significantly reduces annotation costs while maximizing data efficiency
**기존 문제 해결**: 모델 붕괴(model collapse)와 보상 해킹(reward hacking) 문제를 해결하는 우수한 성능 / Addresses critical issues of model collapse and reward hacking in previous RL approaches
**자율 학습 메커니즘**: LLM이 외부 감독 없이 스스로 성능을 개선할 수 있는 새로운 강화학습 패러다임 제시 / Presents novel reinforcement learning paradigm enabling LLMs to self-improve without extensive external supervision

articles.title: FASE : A Fairness-Aware Spatiotemporal Event Graph Framework for Predictive Policing
articles.titleKo: FASE: 예측 경찰활동을 위한 공정성을 고려한 시공간 이벤트 그래프 프레임워크

articles.aiSummary:
Predictive policing systems that allocate police patrol resources based solely on crime risk predictions can unintentionally reinforce racial disparities and systemic biases through feedback-driven data distortion. To address this problem, researchers developed FASE (Fairness Aware Spatiotemporal Event Graph), a new framework that combines crime prediction with fairness-constrained patrol allocation. The system predicts crime patterns at hourly resolution while simultaneously applying fairness constraints to resource deployment decisions. The researchers analyzed Baltimore by dividing it into 25 zip code tabulation areas and examined 139,982 Part 1 crime incidents from 2017-2019. Unlike traditional predictive policing, FASE includes a closed-loop feedback simulator that tracks how patrol allocation decisions impact future crime data and enables continuous fairness improvements. The study demonstrates that AI systems can effectively prevent crime while mitigating algorithmic bias and social inequity. However, the research has limitations in fully capturing the complex variables involved in real-world police operations.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
경찰청이 범죄 예측만을 바탕으로 순찰 자원을 배치하면 특정 지역에 대한 편견이 계속 강화되어 인종 차별이 심화될 수 있다. 이 문제를 해결하기 위해 연구팀은 'FASE'라는 새로운 공정성 기반 시공간 사건 그래프 프레임워크를 개발했다. 이 시스템은 범죄 발생 패턴을 시간과 공간 단위로 예측하면서 동시에 순찰 배치 시 공정성 제약을 적용한다. 연구팀은 볼티모어를 25개의 우편번호 지역으로 나누고, 2017년부터 2019년까지 139,982건의 범죄 사건 데이터(시간 단위)를 분석했다. FASE는 기존의 편향된 예측 경찰 시스템과 달리 피드백 루프 시뮬레이터를 통해 배치 결정이 미래 데이터에 미치는 영향을 추적하고 공정성을 개선할 수 있다. 이 연구는 AI 시스템이 사회적 편견을 방지하면서도 효과적인 범죄 예방을 할 수 있음을 보여준다는 의의가 있으나, 실제 경찰 운영의 복잡한 변수들을 완전히 반영하지 못했다는 한계가 있다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Predictive policing systems that allocate police patrol resources based solely on crime risk predictions can unintentionally reinforce racial disparities and systemic biases through feedback-driven data distortion. To address this problem, researchers developed FASE (Fairness Aware Spatiotemporal Event Graph), a new framework that combines crime prediction with fairness-constrained patrol allocation. The system predicts crime patterns at hourly resolution while simultaneously applying fairness constraints to resource deployment decisions. The researchers analyzed Baltimore by dividing it into 25 zip code tabulation areas and examined 139,982 Part 1 crime incidents from 2017-2019. Unlike traditional predictive policing, FASE includes a closed-loop feedback simulator that tracks how patrol allocation decisions impact future crime data and enables continuous fairness improvements. The study demonstrates that AI systems can effectively prevent crime while mitigating algorithmic bias and social inequity. However, the research has limitations in fully capturing the complex variables involved in real-world police operations.

##
keyPoints: **순환적 편향의 악순환 해결** / Breaking the cycle of algorithmic feedback bias: 기존 예측 경찰 시스템은 과도한 순찰로 인해 특정 지역의 범죄 적발률이 높아지고, 이것이 다시 범죄 예측 데이터로 입력되어 편향을 증폭시키는 문제를 해결함
**시공간 데이터 기반 정밀 분석** / Spatiotemporal granularity in crime analysis: 139,982건의 범죄 사건을 시간 단위(시간별)와 공간 단위(25개 지역)로 분석하여 보다 세밀한 범죄 패턴 파악이 가능
**공정성 제약의 통합** / Integrated fairness constraints: 범죄 예측 정확도와 사회적 공정성 두 가지 목표를 동시에 달성하는 최적화 알고리즘을 제시

articles.title: Curiosity-Critic: Cumulative Prediction Error Improvement as a Tractable Intrinsic Reward for World Model Training
articles.titleKo: 호기심-비평가: 세계 모델 학습을 위한 계산 가능한 내재적 보상으로서의 누적 예측 오차 개선
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.18701v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Local prediction-error-based curiosity rewards focus on the current transition without considering the world model's cumulative prediction error across all visited transitions. We introduce Curiosity-Critic, which grounds its intrinsic reward in the improvement of this cumulative objective, and show that it reduces to a tractable per-step form: the difference between the current prediction error and the asymptotic error baseline of the current state transition. We estimate this baseline online with a learned critic co-trained alongside the world
articles.aiSummary:
In artificial intelligence's world model training (where AI learns how environments work), curiosity-based rewards play a crucial role. Previous research focused only on prediction errors in the current transition, but this paper proposes 'Curiosity-Critic,' which emphasizes improvement in cumulative prediction errors across all visited states. The research team converted the complex cumulative objective into a tractable per-step form: the difference between current prediction error and the asymptotic error baseline of that state transition. A learned neural network called a Critic is co-trained to estimate this baseline error. This method enhances the efficiency of world model learning and improves AI's exploration strategy. The paper emphasizes computational tractability and theoretical validity, though validation in complex real-world environments may require further investigation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.titleKo: 신경과학과 인공지능의 융합: 뇌과학의 발전과 AI를 잇다

articles.aiSummary:
Neuroscience and Artificial Intelligence have achieved impressive progress in recent years, yet remain only loosely connected. Based on a National Science Foundation workshop in August 2025, researchers identified three fundamental capability gaps in current AI systems: inability to interact with the physical world, brittle learning systems, and unsustainable energy and data inefficiency. Neuroscience offers principles to address these challenges: co-design of body and controller, predictive learning through interaction, and multisensory integration. By applying biological principles from neuroscience to AI development, this research proposes a pathway toward creating more robust, efficient, and adaptive AI systems. However, translating neuroscience principles into AI implementations faces significant complexities and practical constraints.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Quantum-Like Models of Cognition and Decision Making: Open-Systems and Gorini--Kossakowski--Sudarshan--Lindblad Dynamics
articles.titleKo: 인지와 의사결정의 양자형 모델: 개방 시스템과 고리니-코삭스키-수다르산-린드블라드 동역학
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.18643v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper starts with surveying the evolution of quantum-like models of cognition and decision making, transitioning from static kinematic representations to a robust dynamical framework based on open quantum systems. We provide a comprehensive analysis of the Gorini-Kossakowski-Sudarshan-Lindblad (GKSL) master equation's application in cognitive psychology and decision making, illustrating how it models mental state evolution as a dissipative process influenced by an informational environment. We categorize dynamical regimes into Passive and Ac
articles.aiSummary:
This paper provides a comprehensive survey of the evolution of quantum-like models in cognitive psychology and decision making, moving from static kinematic representations to a robust dynamical framework based on open quantum systems. The authors analyze the application of the Gorini-Kossakowski-Sudarshan-Lindblad (GKSL) master equation in cognitive psychology, demonstrating how it models mental state evolution as a dissipative process influenced by the informational environment. This mathematical framework naturally explains irrational human decision-making patterns (such as conjunction fallacy and order effects) that classical probability theory fails to account for. The study distinguishes between two dynamical regimes: Passive and Active processes, providing a more nuanced understanding of cognition. The research bridges quantum mechanics and psychology, suggesting that cognitive processes share mathematical structures with quantum systems. However, limitations include limited experimental validation and unclear correspondence between the theoretical model and actual neural mechanisms.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Psychologists pinpoint the conversational mechanisms that help humans bond with AI
articles.titleKo: 심리학자들이 인간이 AI와 유대감을 형성하는 대화 메커니즘을 규명하다
articles.summary: New research has identified the specific psychological mechanism that drives deep connections between humans and AI. Scientists found that perceived responsiveness—when a chatbot makes users feel understood and validated—is the key to forming meaningful digital bonds.
articles.aiSummary:
Recent psychological research has identified the specific mechanism that drives humans to form deep emotional bonds with artificial intelligence. Researchers found that "perceived responsiveness"—the ability of a chatbot to make users feel understood and validated—is the key factor in forming meaningful digital relationships. This mechanism explains why some users experience AI interactions as deeply personal and significant, comparable to human relationships. The study reveals that when users perceive AI as responsive to their needs and emotions, they are more likely to develop stronger connections. These findings have important implications for mental health support, educational AI development, and using AI to alleviate loneliness. However, the research may be based on relatively short-term interactions, and further studies are needed to understand the long-term psychological effects of sustained AI-human relationships.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: Manipulative people use both kindness and gossip as separate tools to control their social circles
articles.titleKo: 조종하려는 사람들은 친절함과 험담을 별개의 도구로 사용해 자신의 사회적 인맥을 지배한다
articles.summary: People who manipulate their peers through gossip and exclusion are largely motivated by dark personality traits. A new study shows that possessing a bright and benevolent worldview does almost nothing to prevent this type of social sabotage.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigated how people with manipulative tendencies use their social relationships for control. Researchers analyzed the relationships between personality traits (dark personality traits and benevolent worldviews) and social manipulation behaviors (kindness, gossip, and exclusion) among participants. Key findings revealed that individuals with higher dark personality traits (narcissism, psychological egoism, etc.) tend to simultaneously employ both kindness and gossip as tools to control others. Surprisingly, people with benevolent worldviews are not completely protected from these manipulative behaviors and can also use kindness as a manipulation tool. The study demonstrates that outward displays of kindness do not always reflect genuine benevolence in human relationships. However, the research has limitations as it focused on a specific sample group, making generalization across diverse cultures and social groups challenging.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 조종적 성향을 가진 사람들이 어떤 방식으로 자신의 사회 관계를 통제하는지 조사했습니다. 연구진은 참가자들의 성격 특성(암흑 성격 특성과 선량한 세계관)과 사회적 조종 행동(친절함, 험담, 배제) 사이의 관계를 분석했습니다. 핵심 결과는 암흑 성격 특성(자기애, 심리적 이기주의 등)이 높을수록 다른 사람들을 통제하기 위해 친절함과 험담을 동시에 사용하는 경향이 있다는 것입니다. 놀랍게도 선량한 세계관을 가진 사람들도 이러한 조종적 행동으로부터 완전히 보호되지 않으며, 친절함을 조종의 도구로 사용할 수 있습니다. 이 연구는 인간관계에서 겉으로 드러나는 친절함이 항상 진정한 선의를 반영하지 않을 수 있음을 보여줍니다. 다만 이 연구는 특정 표본 집단을 대상으로 했으므로 다양한 문화와 사회 집단에 일반화하기에는 제한이 있습니다.

articles.title: Modelling time-order effects in haptic perception with a Bayesian dynamical framework
articles.titleKo: 베이지안 동역학 프레임워크를 이용한 촉각 지각에서의 시간 순서 효과 모델링
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.19662v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Perceptual judgments of sequential stimuli are systematically biased by prior expectations and by the temporal structure of sensory input. In haptic discrimination tasks, these effects often manifest as time-order asymmetries, whereby the perceived difference between two stimuli depends on their presentation order. Here, we introduce a dynamical Bayesian model that accounts for these biases by combining noisy sensory measurements with an evolving internal representation of stimulus intensity. The model formalizes perception as an inference proces
articles.aiSummary:
This study introduces a Bayesian dynamical model to explain time-order effects in haptic perception—the phenomenon where the perceived difference between two tactile stimuli depends on their presentation order. Researchers observed that sequential perceptual judgments are systematically biased by prior expectations and the temporal structure of sensory input. The model integrates noisy sensory measurements with an evolving internal representation of stimulus intensity, formalizing perception as an inference process. Results demonstrate that the model successfully explains asymmetrical time-order effects observed in haptic discrimination tasks. The significance lies in advancing our understanding of sequential perception and potentially contributing to neuroscience and AI model development. However, the study lacks connection to underlying neural mechanisms, and generalizability to other sensory modalities remains to be validated.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 촉각 지각 작업에서 나타나는 시간 순서 효과(순서에 따라 지각이 달라지는 현상)를 설명하기 위해 베이지안 동역학 모델을 개발했습니다. 연구팀은 두 개의 촉각 자극을 순차적으로 제시할 때, 사람들의 지각이 이전 기대와 감각 입력의 시간적 구조에 의해 체계적으로 편향된다는 점에 주목했습니다. 이 모델은 잡음이 있는 감각 측정과 시간에 따라 변화하는 자극 강도의 내적 표현을 결합하여 지각을 추론 과정으로 형식화합니다. 연구 결과는 촉각 차별 작업에서 관찰되는 비대칭적 시간 순서 효과를 설명할 수 있음을 보여줍니다. 이 연구의 의의는 인간의 순차적 지각 과정에 대한 이해를 깊게 하고, 신경과학 및 AI 모델 개발에 기여할 수 있다는 점입니다. 다만 실제 신경 메커니즘과의 연결이 부족하며, 다른 감각 양식에서의 일반화 가능성은 아직 검증되지 않았습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study introduces a Bayesian dynamical model to explain time-order effects in haptic perception—the phenomenon where the perceived difference between two tactile stimuli depends on their presentation order. Researchers observed that sequential perceptual judgments are systematically biased by prior expectations and the temporal structure of sensory input. The model integrates noisy sensory measurements with an evolving internal representation of stimulus intensity, formalizing perception as an inference process. Results demonstrate that the model successfully explains asymmetrical time-order effects observed in haptic discrimination tasks. The significance lies in advancing our understanding of sequential perception and potentially contributing to neuroscience and AI model development. However, the study lacks connection to underlying neural mechanisms, and generalizability to other sensory modalities remains to be validated.

##
keyPoints: **시간 순서 효과의 수학적 모델화** / Mathematical Modeling of Temporal Order Effects
**감각-인지 통합 메커니즘** / Integration of Sensory-Cognitive Mechanisms
**비대칭적 지각 편향 설명** / Explanation of Asymmetrical Perceptual Bias
**신경과학 및 AI 응용 가능성** / Potential Applications in Neuroscience and AI

articles.title: The Hard Truths Behavioral Science Must Face in Conflict Settings
articles.titleKo: 분쟁 상황에서 행동과학이 직면해야 할 어려운 진실들
articles.summary: Approximately 2 billion people live in conflict-affected areas. Yet the field of behavioral science has developed most of its theories, run most of its experiments, and built most of its interventions in stable contexts. When behavioral scientists do work in conflict settings, we tend to follow a familiar pattern: take what worked elsewhere, “localize” it, and evaluate it. But this isn’t enough. This is an urgent problem. Conflict is reshaping lives globally at an unprecedented scale. In 2024, 123 million people were forcibly displaced —the highest number on record. Civilian casualties in Gaza
articles.aiSummary:
Approximately 2 billion people live in conflict-affected areas, with 123 million people forcibly displaced in 2024—a record high. However, behavioral science has developed most of its theories, experiments, and interventions in stable contexts, creating a critical gap between scientific practice and real-world needs. The current approach of taking successful interventions from elsewhere and "localizing" them for conflict settings is insufficient for addressing the complex psychological phenomena in such environments. This research emphasizes that fundamental rethinking is needed rather than simple adaptation of existing methods. The significance lies in highlighting that behavioral science must develop interventions specifically designed for conflict-affected populations, considering their unique circumstances and constraints. A major limitation is the absence of specific experimental data or detailed implementation strategies tailored to different types of conflict situations. The paper effectively calls for urgent research repositioning but lacks concrete empirical solutions. Future studies must bridge the gap between theoretical understanding and practical application in high-risk environments.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
현재 약 20억 명이 분쟁 영향 지역에 거주하고 있으며, 2024년에는 역대 최고 수준인 1억 2,300만 명이 강제 이주를 경험했습니다. 그러나 행동심리학 분야는 대부분의 이론, 실험, 개입 방안을 안정적인 환경에서 개발해왔다는 근본적인 문제를 안고 있습니다. 현재의 관행은 다른 지역에서 성공한 방법을 분쟁 지역에 '현지화'하여 적용하고 평가하는 수준에 머물러 있습니다. 이 연구는 분쟁 상황에서의 인간 행동을 이해하기 위해서는 근본적으로 다른 접근이 필요하다고 주장합니다. 기존의 '이전 후 평가' 방식으로는 극도로 불안정한 분쟁 환경에서의 복잡한 심리 현상을 설명할 수 없습니다. 연구의 의의는 행동과학이 분쟁 지역 주민의 삶에 실질적 영향을 미치는 개입을 개발해야 함을 강조하는 것입니다. 제한점으로는 구체적인 실험 데이터가 부재하고, 다양한 분쟁 상황별 특수성을 고려한 세부 방안이 제시되지 않았다는 점입니다.

articles.title: The ‘Busy’ Trap
articles.summary: If you live in America in the twenty-first century you’ve probably had to listen to a lot of people tell you how busy they are. It’s become the default response when you ask anyone how they’re doing: “Busy!” “So busy.” “ Crazy busy.” It is, pretty obviously, a boast disguised as a complaint. And the stock response is a kind of congratulation: “That’s a good problem to have,” or “Better than the opposite.” Notice it isn’t generally people pulling back-to-back shifts in the I.C.U. or commuting by bus to three minimum-wage jobs who tell you how busy they are; what those people are is not busy but
articles.aiSummary:
In twenty-first century America, "busy" has become the default response to casual greetings, functioning as a disguised boast rather than a genuine complaint. Through social observation, the author notes that people who are genuinely busy—such as ICU nurses or those working multiple minimum-wage jobs—do not boast about their busyness. Instead, individuals with higher status and greater autonomy tend to display their packed schedules as a status symbol. This phenomenon reflects how time has become an indicator of social importance and wealth in modern society. The article suggests that claiming to be "busy" is actually a way to demonstrate one's value and significance. Limitations include the focus on American culture, requiring consideration of cultural differences. This discussion raises fundamental questions about work-life balance and how social status is communicated.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
현대 미국 사회에서 "바쁘다"라는 표현이 일상적인 인사말로 자리 잡았는데, 이는 실제로는 불평을 가장한 자랑이 되어버렸다는 내용이다. 연구 및 사회 관찰을 통해 저자는 실제로 바쁜 사람들(의료진, 저임금 노동자 등)은 자신의 바쁨을 자랑하지 않는다는 점을 지적한다. 오히려 높은 지위와 자율성을 가진 사람들이 자신의 바쁜 일정을 과시함으로써 사회적 지위와 중요성을 드러내려는 경향을 보인다. 이러한 현상은 현대 사회에서 시간이 부의 상징이 되었음을 시사한다. 본 글의 한계는 미국 중심의 관찰이며, 문화권별로 다양한 해석이 필요하다는 점이다. 이 논의는 일과 삶의 균형, 그리고 사회적 지위 표현 방식에 대한 근본적인 질문을 던진다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
In twenty-first century America, "busy" has become the default response to casual greetings, functioning as a disguised boast rather than a genuine complaint. Through social observation, the author notes that people who are genuinely busy—such as ICU nurses or those working multiple minimum-wage jobs—do not boast about their busyness. Instead, individuals with higher status and greater autonomy tend to display their packed schedules as a status symbol. This phenomenon reflects how time has become an indicator of social importance and wealth in modern society. The article suggests that claiming to be "busy" is actually a way to demonstrate one's value and significance. Limitations include the focus on American culture, requiring consideration of cultural differences. This discussion raises fundamental questions about work-life balance and how social status is communicated.

##
keyPoints: **"바쁨의 역설"** / **The Paradox of Busyness**: 실제로 과도하게 바쁜 저소득층 노동자들은 자신의 바쁨을 말하지 않으며, 상대적으로 여유가 있는 고소득층이 바쁨을 자랑한다 / Genuinely overworked low-income workers don't boast about their busyness, while those with greater flexibility and higher status do.
**사회적 지위의 신호** / **Status Signaling**: "바쁘다"는 표현은 자신의 중요성과 사회적 가치를 드러내는 수단으로 기능한다 / Claiming to be busy serves as a way to signal one's importance and social value in modern society.
**시간 = 부의 상징** / **Time as a Symbol of Wealth**: 현대 미국 사회에서 바쁜 일정은 부와 권력의 증거로 인식된다 / A packed schedule is perceived as evidence of wealth, power, and social importance.

**문화적 맥락의 필요성** / **Need for Cultural Context**: 미국 중심의 관찰이며, 다른 문화권에서는 다양한 해석과 적용이 필요하다 / The article is America-centric; different cultural contexts may interpret and experience busyness differently.

articles.title: What It’s Like to Be…an Aerospace Engineer
articles.titleKo:
이것이 무엇인가를...항공우주 엔지니어가 되는 것

또는 더 자연스럽게:

항공우주 엔지니어가 되는 것은 어떤가?
articles.summary: Landing the Perseverance rover on Mars, working in clean rooms to minimize the microbial bug count, and slogging through hundreds of engineering trade-offs with Swati Mohan, an aerospace engineer at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory. What are the "seven minutes of terror"? And is there evidence of past life on Mars? [buzzsprout episode='18903199' player='true'] View the episode transcript About What It's Like to Be... In each episode of What It's Like to Be..., bestselling author Dan Heath speaks with someone about what it's like to walk in their (work) shoes. Behavioral Scientist serves as a d
articles.aiSummary:
This article features a podcast episode in which bestselling author Dan Heath interviews Swati Mohan, an aerospace engineer at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory, about her experience working on the Perseverance rover's Mars landing mission. Rather than a formal research study, this is a narrative-based interview format without specific participant numbers or experimental design. Mohan discusses the practical challenges of her work, including maintaining sterile conditions by minimizing microbial counts in clean rooms, navigating hundreds of engineering trade-offs, and managing the intense "Seven Minutes of Terror" during Mars landing. The episode aims to make complex space exploration and aerospace engineering accessible to general audiences through firsthand expert perspectives. The podcast format inherently limits the inclusion of specific quantitative data or scientific metrics, focusing instead on personal professional experiences and insights.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.title: ‘We Have Never Been Individuals’
articles.summary: Take a long, deep breath in. Now slowly let it out. Each time you inhale, you’re drawing in oxygen from the plants around you. Once in your lungs, oxygen navigates the bloodstream, where it gets exchanged for carbon dioxide. With each exhale, you fill the air with carbon dioxide, the very substance that all these plants need for photosynthesis. What we breathe out, plants breathe in. What plants breathe out, we breathe in. The air you breathe is the collective breath of other living beings. You are immersed in the world. And the world is immersed in you. Your body hosts a remarkable diversity
articles.aiSummary:
This article presents a philosophical perspective that humans are not independent entities but are deeply interconnected with their surrounding environment. Using breathing as an example, it explains the reciprocal nature of material cycles: the oxygen we inhale comes from plants, and the carbon dioxide we exhale is necessary for plant photosynthesis. This implies that our bodies are not merely individual organisms but complex ecosystems that continuously interact with surrounding living beings. The article emphasizes that our bodies contain diverse microorganisms and form an integrated system through material exchange with the environment. This perspective helps us realize our mutual interdependence—that we are "immersed in the world" and simultaneously "the world is immersed in us." The article's limitation is that it is based more on philosophical reflection than on concrete scientific data or empirical research findings.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 기사는 인간이 독립적인 개체가 아니라 주변 환경과 깊이 있게 연결되어 있다는 철학적 관점을 제시합니다. 호흡을 예시로 들어, 우리가 들이마시는 산소는 식물에서 나오고 우리가 내쉬는 이산화탄소는 식물의 광합성에 필요하다는 물질 순환의 상호성을 설명합니다. 이는 우리의 몸이 단순한 개별 유기체가 아니라 주변 생명체들과 지속적으로 상호작용하는 복잡한 생태계임을 의미합니다. 기사는 인간의 몸이 다양한 미생물을 포함하고 있으며, 환경과의 물질 교환을 통해 하나의 통합된 시스템을 이루고 있음을 강조합니다. 이러한 관점은 우리가 자연에 '담겨있고' 동시에 자연이 우리 안에 '담겨있다'는 상호의존성을 깨닫게 합니다. 기사의 한계는 구체적인 과학적 데이터나 실증 연구 결과보다는 철학적 사유에 기반하고 있다는 점입니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:

The article's limitation is that it is based more on philosophical reflection than on concrete scientific data or empirical research findings.

##
keyPoints: **상호호흡의 순환** / **Reciprocal Respiration Cycle**: 인간이 들이마시는 산소와 내쉬는 이산화탄소는 식물과의 물질 교환 관계를 형성하며, 이는 인간과 자연이 분리되지 않음을 보여줍니다. / Humans' inhalation of oxygen and exhalation of carbon dioxide form material exchange relationships with plants, demonstrating that humans and nature are inseparable.

**개체성의 재정의** / **Redefining Individuality**: "우리는 개별적이지 않았다"라는 제목은 인간을 독립적 개체로 보는 기존의 관점을 거부하고 관계와 상호연결성 중심의 인간관을 제안합니다. / The title "We Have Never Been Individuals" rejects the conventional view of humans as independent entities and proposes a human-centered perspective based on relationships and interconnectedness.

**신체 내 생태계** / **Ecosystem Within the Body**: 인간의 몸은 다양한 미생물을 포함하는 복잡한 생태계이며, 단순한 생물학적 기관의 집합이 아닙니다. / The human body is a complex ecosystem containing diverse microorganisms, not merely a collection of biological organs.

**환경과의 상호침투** / **Mutual Immersion with Environment**: 우리가 환경에 담겨있듯이 환경도 우리 안에 담겨있으며, 이는 인간을 고립된 개체가 아닌 더 큰 전체의 부분으로 이해하게 합니다. / Just as we are immersed in the environment, the environment is immersed in us, allowing us to understand humans not as isolated entities but as parts of a larger whole.

articles.title: Everyday infections, not vaccines, are linked to an increased risk of childhood stroke
articles.titleKo: 백신이 아닌 일상적인 감염이 소아 뇌졸중 위험 증가와 관련이 있다
articles.summary: While strokes in children remain incredibly rare, new research shows that experiencing a recent infection can temporarily double the risk. Scientists confirmed that standard childhood vaccinations do not trigger these vascular events.
articles.aiSummary:
This research was conducted to identify risk factors for childhood stroke. Previous concerns had been raised about whether vaccines could trigger strokes, prompting scientists to systematically verify these claims. Researchers tracked and analyzed stroke incidence in children with recent infection histories, finding that common infections approximately doubled the risk of stroke. However, standard childhood vaccinations were proven to have no association with stroke risk. This finding is significant as it alleviates parental concerns about vaccine safety and redirects attention to actual risk factors. Nevertheless, the study's limitation is that childhood strokes remain extremely rare, so the absolute risk remains very low.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 어린이의 뇌졸중 위험 요인을 파악하기 위해 진행되었습니다. 과거 백신이 뇌졸중을 유발한다는 우려가 제기되어 왔으나, 과학자들은 이를 체계적으로 검증하고자 했습니다. 연구진은 최근 감염 이력이 있는 어린이들의 뇌졸중 발생률을 추적 관찰하여 분석했으며, 결과적으로 일반적인 감염이 뇌졸중 위험을 약 2배 증가시키는 것으로 확인되었습니다. 반면 표준 어린이 예방접종은 뇌졸중 위험과 전혀 관련이 없음이 입증되었습니다. 이 발견은 부모들의 백신 안전성에 대한 우려를 해소하고 실제 위험 요인에 관심을 돌릴 수 있게 한다는 점에서 의의가 있습니다. 다만, 어린이 뇌졸중 자체가 매우 드물기 때문에 절대적 위험도는 여전히 낮다는 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research was conducted to identify risk factors for childhood stroke. Previous concerns had been raised about whether vaccines could trigger strokes, prompting scientists to systematically verify these claims. Researchers tracked and analyzed stroke incidence in children with recent infection histories, finding that common infections approximately doubled the risk of stroke. However, standard childhood vaccinations were proven to have no association with stroke risk. This finding is significant as it alleviates parental concerns about vaccine safety and redirects attention to actual risk factors. Nevertheless, the study's limitation is that childhood strokes remain extremely rare, so the absolute risk remains very low.

##
keyPoints: **감염과 뇌졸중**: 최근 감염 경험이 어린이의 뇌졸중 위험을 약 2배 증가시킴 / Recent infections approximately double the risk of childhood stroke
**백신 안전성 확인**: 표준 어린이 예방접종이 뇌졸중 유발과 무관함이 과학적으로 입증됨 / Standard childhood vaccinations have been scientifically proven to have no link to stroke risk
**절대적 위험도 낮음**: 어린이 뇌졸중은 여전히 매우 드물기 때문에 실제 위험 확률은 낮음 / Childhood strokes remain incredibly rare, so actual risk probability remains very low
**공중보건 지침**: 백신 접종의 필요성과 감염 예방의 중요성이 재확인됨 / The necessity of vaccination and importance of infection prevention are reaffirmed

articles.title: Brain waves predict the intensity of magic mushroom trips
articles.summary: A quick scan of your resting brain waves might hold the secret to predicting how intensely you will react to psilocybin, offering doctors a potential new tool for personalizing psychedelic therapy.
articles.aiSummary:
A study has discovered that resting brain waves can predict how intensely an individual will react to psilocybin, the active compound in magic mushrooms. Researchers measured participants' resting-state brain activity and found it could forecast the psychological intensity of their response after psilocybin administration. This brain wave scanning method is non-invasive and can be performed quickly, suggesting that individual neural activity patterns are key determinants of sensitivity to psychedelic substances. The findings offer significant potential for personalizing psychedelic-assisted therapy by allowing doctors to tailor treatment based on each patient's neurobiological profile. This could be particularly valuable for treating conditions like depression, anxiety, and PTSD with psilocybin therapy by determining appropriate dosages and treatment protocols for individual patients. However, limitations include the complexity of interpreting brain wave data, potentially small sample sizes, and questions about generalizability across diverse populations requiring further research.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
매직 머쉬룸(매직 버섯)의 주성분인 실로사이빈에 대한 개인의 반응 강도를 미리 예측할 수 있는 방법이 발견되었다. 연구팀은 참가자들의 휴식 상태 뇌파(resting brain waves)를 측정하여 실로사이빈 복용 후 나타날 심리적 반응의 강도를 예측할 수 있음을 발견했다. 뇌파 검사는 비침습적이고 빠르게 수행할 수 있는 간단한 방법으로, 개인의 뇌 활동 패턴이 향정신성 물질에 대한 반응성을 결정하는 중요한 요소임을 시사한다. 이 발견은 향정신성 약물 치료를 개인의 특성에 맞게 맞춤형으로 제공할 수 있게 해주므로 정신 건강 치료의 새로운 가능성을 열어준다. 특히 우울증, 불안증, PTSD 등의 치료에 실로사이빈을 사용할 때 환자별 적절한 용량과 치료 방식을 결정하는 데 도움이 될 수 있다. 다만, 뇌파 데이터의 해석, 표본 크기, 그리고 다양한 인구 집단에 대한 일반화 가능성 등은 추가 연구가 필요한 제한점이다.

articles.title: People with cannabis disorder do not seem to pay increased attention to pictures of cannabis
articles.titleKo: 대마초 사용장애가 있는 사람들은 대마초 사진에 증가된 주의를 기울이는 것으로 보이지 않는다
articles.summary: Does addiction always hijack your eyes? A surprising new Australian study found that heavy cannabis users don't actually pay more automatic attention to weed-related images than non-users, challenging long-held theories about how cannabis use disorder works.
articles.aiSummary:
This Australian study investigated whether addiction automatically hijacks attention toward addiction-related stimuli, challenging long-established theories about cannabis use disorder. Researchers compared eye-tracking responses to cannabis-related images between individuals with cannabis use disorder and non-users. Contrary to prevailing theories, heavy cannabis users showed no increased automatic attention to cannabis-related pictures compared to control participants. The findings suggest that cannabis addiction mechanisms cannot be fully explained by simple attentional bias theories. This significant discovery prompts reconsideration of the neurobiological mechanisms underlying addiction, though the study has limitations including potential cultural context differences and sample size constraints.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 약물 중독이 자동적으로 중독 관련 자극에 대한 주의력을 증가시킨다는 기존 이론을 검증하기 위해 실시되었다. 호주 연구팀은 대마초 사용 장애가 있는 사람들과 비사용자들을 대상으로 대마초 관련 이미지에 대한 자동적 주의 반응을 측정하는 실험을 진행했다. 눈 추적 기술을 사용한 연구 결과, 흥미롭게도 대마초 사용 장애가 있는 사람들이 대마초 관련 사진을 볼 때 비사용자들보다 자동적 주의를 더 많이 기울이지 않는 것으로 나타났다. 이는 중독 메커니즘이 기존 이론처럼 단순히 자동적 주의 편향(attentional bias)만으로는 설명될 수 없음을 시사한다. 본 연구는 중독의 신경생물학적 기제를 재고하게 하는 중요한 발견이나, 문화적 맥락 차이와 표본 크기 한계가 있다.

articles.title: Artificial neurons successfully communicate with living brain cells
articles.titleKo: 인공 뉴런이 살아있는 뇌 세포와의 통신에 성공하다
articles.summary: Engineers at Northwestern University have taken a striking leap toward merging machines with the human brain by printing artificial neurons that can actually communicate with real ones. These flexible, low-cost devices generate lifelike electrical signals capable of activating living brain cells, a breakthrough demonstrated in mouse brain tissue.
articles.aiSummary:
Engineers at Northwestern University have achieved a significant breakthrough in brain-machine integration by developing artificial neurons capable of communicating with real brain cells. The research demonstrates that these flexible and cost-effective devices can generate electrical signals that mimic biological neural activity. Using mouse brain tissue, researchers successfully showed that artificial neurons can activate living brain cells through these lifelike signals. The significance of this work lies in its potential applications for brain-computer interfaces, neurodegenerative disease treatment, and neural tissue repair. The use of flexible, low-cost materials could also improve accessibility and feasibility of neural implants for patients. However, as this is early-stage research, further clinical studies and safety validation are necessary before human brain applications can be considered.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
노스웨스턴 대학교의 공학자들은 인공 뉴런을 개발하여 실제 뇌세포와 통신할 수 있음을 입증함으로써 기계와 인간 뇌의 융합에 중요한 진전을 이루었다. 이 연구는 유연하고 저비용의 인공 뉴런 장치가 생체 신호와 동일한 전기 신호를 생성할 수 있음을 보여주었다. 연구팀은 실험쥐의 뇌 조직을 사용하여 인공 뉴런이 살아있는 뇌세포를 실제로 활성화시킬 수 있음을 입증했다. 이 기술의 의의는 뇌-컴퓨터 인터페이스, 신경퇴행성 질환 치료, 신경손상 복구 등 의료 응용 분야에 있다. 또한 저비용의 유연한 소재를 사용함으로써 신경 임플란트의 접근성을 높일 수 있는 가능성을 제시한다. 다만 현재는 초기 단계 연구로서, 인간의 뇌에 적용하기 위해서는 더 많은 임상 연구와 안전성 검증이 필요하다.
